about.title: About Borna Art
about.paragraph1: BORNA (Suzana Bornarova), born in 1974 in Skopje, is an abstract artist whose work is defined by intuition, emotion, and bold expression.
about.paragraph2: Raised in a family of painters, her connection to art began early and evolved into a deeply personal visual language. Today, alongside her academic role as a professor at the Faculty of Philosophy at the University “St. Cyril and Methodius” in Skopje, she continues to explore painting as a form of freedom - of thought, feeling, and spirit.
about.paragraph3: Her work is characterized by vibrant color contrasts, dynamic textures, and expressive movement. Using acrylics, palette knife techniques, structural paste, and impasto, BORNA creates layered compositions that balance abstraction with subtle figurative elements.
about.paragraph4: Each piece is created spontaneously, without sketches - guided purely by instinct.
about.paragraph5: Every BORNA artwork is an original. No copies. No reproductions. Just one-of-a-kind expressions - impossible to replicate, and instantly recognizable.
about.statementParagraph1: BORNA's artworks are never printed. She works with high-quality acrylic paints, mediums, structural pastes, and finishing varnishes sourced from American, German, Polish, English, Dutch, and Italian manufacturers to create original one-of-a-kind pieces. To protect each painting from sunlight and other outside influences, and to ensure easier care and long-term durability, every work is sealed with a final protective varnish.
about.statementParagraph2: The paintings are created on premium 100% cotton canvas stretched on wooden frames or panels of Italian, Greek, Serbian, and local origin. After framing, each artwork is carefully packed first in bubble wrap and then in cardboard so it can be delivered safely to your home. To date, not a single BORNA painting has been damaged in transit.
about.processIntro: Switch between three real five-step painting sequences from the atelier.

step1.title: Palette
step1.description: Choosing the dominant colors and mood of the composition.

step2.title: Surface
step2.description: Preparing the canvas with texture and the first base layers.

step3.title: Movement
step3.description: Building gesture, motion, and balance across the composition.

step4.title: Depth
step4.description: Adding structural paste, contrast, and layered highlights.

step5.title: Finish
step5.description: Final review, detailing, and protective varnish application.

step1.title: Reference
step1.description: Collecting emotional cues, tones, and visual inspiration.

step2.title: Sketchless Start
step2.description: Beginning directly on canvas without a preparatory drawing.

step3.title: Texture
step3.description: Working the knife and paste to create tactile surfaces.

step4.title: Contrast
step4.description: Balancing bold accents with calmer negative space.

step5.title: Seal
step5.description: Sealing the final painting for longevity and safe display.

step1.title: Impulse
step1.description: Starting from instinct and emotional rhythm.

step2.title: Layer One
step2.description: Establishing broad forms and key tonal anchors.

step3.title: Layer Two
step3.description: Introducing line, edge, and figurative tension.

step4.title: Refinement
step4.description: Adjusting color relationships and compositional flow.

step5.title: Presentation
step5.description: Preparing the work for framing, packing, and delivery.
